summary: Set 070 Session 2 of 3 -- provenance merge + the fair-shake telemetry + dual-surface mode wiring. Ran on claude-opus-4-8 high. All in ai_router/dual_surface_verify.py (extending the S1 two-arm runner) + a JSON Schema + a test file. DELIVERED: (1) PROVENANCE MERGE -- merge_findings/MergedFinding/MergeResult: two findings merge to 'both' ONLY when they share a non-empty explicit defectKey, NEVER on free-text wording (the Set 069 S6 floor-ratchet lesson that a description is not an identity). The SAFE DIRECTION is enforced: an unkeyed defect both arms caught becomes TWO single-surface entries (over-split, conservative -- it never HIDES a push-unique catch, which would bias RETIRE toward retiring push / 'throwing out the baby'), and the result honestly flags provenance_complete=False + per-surface unkeyed counts. Intra-arm duplicate keys fold to one contributor set (no double-count); severity=most-severe across contributors; both arms' wording preserved in contributors. (2) COMPARISON ARTIFACT + VALIDATOR + JSON SCHEMA -- build_comparison_artifact, validate_comparison_artifact, docs/dual-surface-comparison.schema.json + docs/dual-surface-comparison-schema-example.json. Pure-Python validator in L-066-1 PARITY with the schema: closed top-level/contributor/merged-finding key sets, int-not-bool guards (schemaVersion, pushUnkeyed, pullUnkeyed), typed optionals, AND the cross-field provenance invariants the schema cannot express (a 'both' finding's contributors must cover both surfaces AND carry a defectKey; a push-only/pull-only's contributors are exactly that surface; provenanceComplete=true is inconsistent with any unkeyed finding OR a nonzero unkeyed count). Example validates under BOTH jsonschema and the Python validator. (3) FAIR-SHAKE SCORING -- score_comparison (push-unique/pull-unique/shared HIGH-SEVERITY tally, reported as an UPPER BOUND when provenance is incomplete), score_against_benchmark (the RETIRE telemetry over the Set 069 pre-registered seeded+holdout benchmark via replacement_gate.validate_benchmark_registration; ground truth = defectKey is a registered case; underpowered -> INCONCLUSIVE even when push_unique>0; unkeyed high-sev EXCLUDED from the real tally; the gated push layer is NEVER retired here -- the verdict is a recommendation toward the operator-confirmed decision), aggregate_retire_telemetry (REFUSES to pool 'sampled' with 'opt-in' -- the honesty standard has teeth). (4) DUAL-SURFACE MODE + CLI -- dualSurfaceMode (off/sampled/opt-in) recorded once-at-set-start + immutable in activity-log.json (mirrors Set 057 verification_mode / Set 066 pathAwareCritique; distinct entry kind so it overloads neither enum); read/has/unreadable/record/resolve_and_record/spec-seed readers that NEVER raise on a corrupt/malformed log; should_run_dual_surface with the random draw INJECTED (hermetic, deterministic) -- off never runs, opt-in only on explicit request, sampled fires when sample_value<sample_rate (tag 'sampled') but a deliberate opt-in under sampled mode is the OPERATIONAL 'opt-in' tag (never folded into unbiased telemetry); CLI python -m ai_router.dual_surface_verify record-mode/read-mode/score. VERIFICATION (cross-provider, gpt-5-4, routed_gate -> REQUIRED via blast-radius cross-artifact + shared-schema + multi-module; up-front conventions block per L-064-10; strong devil's-advocate framing): R1 ISSUES_FOUND x3 (all REAL, no false positives) -- (a, High) validator accepted provenanceComplete=true with nonzero unkeyed COUNTS when no unkeyed FINDING present -> score_comparison would clear the upper-bound honesty warning -> fixed via an unconditional count-consistency check + score derives completeness from flag AND counts; (b, Med) record-mode could crash on a malformed activity log -> hardened record_dual_surface_mode (controlled ValueError on unparseable/non-object, repair non-list entries) + main() pre-checks unreadable and broadens its except; (c, Med) two tests didn't exercise the behavior they named -> fixed. R2 ISSUES_FOUND x2 -- Fix 2 was an INCOMPLETE class-fix (L-069-1): the READERS (read_/has_) and the stepNumber int() cast could still TypeError on a non-list entries / a malformed stepNumber. Hardened EVERY reader (guard entries is a list before iterating) + filtered prior stepNumbers through _is_int_not_bool + TypeError belt-and-suspenders in the CLI, with the exact malformed-shape regressions R2 named. R3 VERIFIED -- confirmed no remaining traceback path and that the new tests traverse the previously-broken CLI path, not just the writer in isolation. (The R2 first attempt failed with an Anthropic 529 because max_tier=2 had been pinned, dropping the verifier to a same-provider tier-2 model -- a misapply of L-064-7, which is for WORDING-ONLY re-verifies; a substantive re-verify must stay on the R1 verifier's tier. Re-run at tier 3 kept it on gpt-5-4. Noted for a lesson refinement.) DEFERRED RESIDUAL (explicitly recorded per L-069-1): the same non-list-entries iteration pattern exists in the PRE-EXISTING sibling readers ai_router/path_aware_critique.py (~lines 145,209) and ai_router/dedicated_verification.py (~lines 178,243); out of S2 scope (S2 owns dual_surface_verify.py), candidate hardening for S3 or a follow-up -- a decision, not an oversight. SUITE: full ai_router GREEN 2063 passed / 5 skipped (S1 baseline 1988/5 + 75 net new tests; the 5 skips are the Set 069 S4 real-podman-on-Windows by-design skips). No release this session (the ai_router bump + PyPI publish + synthesis docs + live dogfood are S3). Metered spend: verify R1 $0.307 + R2 failed-529 ~$0 + R2-rerun $0.249 + R3 $0.188 + next-orch analysis $0.0025 = ~$0.747. Session 2 VERIFIED.

reason.specifics: For Session 3 of 3 (FINAL: synthesis docs + PyPI release + change-log + next-set recommendation + DOGFOOD the dual-surface mode over this set's own diff + close). Routed analysis (s2-next-orchestrator.md) recommends claude/anthropic/opus-4-8 at medium effort: S3 requires high-fidelity prose synthesis (verification-surface-strategy.md + pull-verifier.md, cross-referenced -- L-065-1 echo-propagation risk), careful execution of the IRREVERSIBLE PyPI release (green-Test-on-the-tagged-SHA; verify tag commit == fixed SHA, the Set 068 lesson; operator pushes/approves the tag), and the live dogfood run of the new mode -- high-stakes, precision-sensitive work where opus-4-8's reasoning earns its cost, and it stays a different provider from the gpt-5-4 verifier. ORCHESTRATOR NOTE: opus-4-8 carried S1+S2; continuing it on the release/dogfood session is consistent. The operator decides; a non-anthropic orchestrator (codex/gpt-5-4 or gemini-pro) is defensible if a fresh perspective on the synthesis docs is preferred, but it would then coincide with the verifier provider.
